subject: Physics
overall_learning_objectives: Analyse pressure as force per unit area with practical examples
Investigate how pressure varies with area and depth
Explain liquid pressure and atmospheric pressure using experiments
Analyse wind formation using pressure differences
Explain high-speed wind effects on pressure
Describe thunderstorms and lightning formation scientifically
Explain cyclone formation using pressure and wind patterns
Evaluate safety measures during storms and cyclones

lesson_plan.title: Concept of Force and Pressure
topics_covered: Applications of pressure in daily life
student_activities: Explain why pointed objects cut more easily
Analyse pressure differences in everyday situations

lesson_plan.title: Pressure and Area Investigation
topics_covered: Applications such as camel feet and broad straps
student_activities: Compare pressure using pointed and blunt objects

lesson_plan.title: Liquid Pressure Experiment
student_activities: Measure distance of water flow from each hole

lesson_plan.title: Atmospheric Pressure Investigation
student_activities: Analyse how atmospheric pressure balances body pressure

lesson_plan.title: Formation of Winds
topics_covered: Air movement from high pressure to low pressure
student_activities: Draw and explain sea breeze and land breeze diagrams
Explain wind direction using pressure differences

lesson_plan.title: High-Speed Winds and Pressure
student_activities: Explain pressure reduction in fast-moving air

lesson_plan.title: Cyclones and Disaster Preparedness
student_activities: Research a recent cyclone in India
Present safety measures and disaster preparedness

teaching_aids: Plastic bottle for liquid pressure experiment
Charts of wind circulation and cyclones
teacher_tip: Encourage students to perform experiments such as the bottle pressure test and balloon airflow activity to help them understand the relationship between pressure, wind movement, and natural weather events.
